title: The Temporal Equivalence Principle: Density-Dependent Screening in Gaia DR3 Wide Binaries
description: A comprehensive analysis of Gaia DR3 wide binaries revealing density-dependent gravitational screening consistent with the Temporal Equivalence Principle. From 341,315 high-purity systems, this work identifies a screening transition at 2646±182 AU, with the velocity profile saturating 35-40% above Newtonian at large separations. The analysis demonstrates environmental ordering required by TEP, with lower-density populations transitioning at smaller radii than higher-density systems.
notes: Part of the TEP Series: Paper 15 (Wide Binaries). This work presents evidence for the Temporal Equivalence Principle through analysis of wide binary systems in the Gaia DR3 catalog.
